rulings.Case Title: WOOD ROBBINS, LLP VS. GMG JANITORIAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Confirm Arb
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER WOOD ROBBINS, LLP'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner Wood Robbins, LLP's motion to confirm arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. Wood Robbins is entitled to judgment in its favor and against respondents GMG Janitorial, Inc. and Gina Gregori in the principal amount of $36,490.01 plus costs per the memorandum of costs procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Wood Robbins is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and a proposed judgment that conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ZAKARIA SAIDIN VS. IRA SCHANZER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Request To Enter Default And Vacate Default Judgement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT IRA SCHANZER'S Motion To Set Aside Request To Enter Default And Vacate Default Judgement. Defendant Ira Schanzer's motion to set aside the August 19, 2014 default and the March 24, 2015 default judgment entered against him is granted. Due process demands, and the doctrine of extrinsic mistake permits, the vacation of the default and default judgment based on the undisputed fact that Mr. Schanzer had no knowledge of this case until November 2018 and that service by publication occurred without plaintiff Zakaria Saidin exhausting all possible sources to be able to serve Mr. Schanzer personally or by substitute service. Mr. Schanzer has shown that due to his "excusable neglect" of not receiving notice of this case by the service by publication he failed to timely appear to prevent the entry of his default and default judgment. Mr. Schanzer has submitted more than ample evidence to satisfy the three part test to vacate a default and default judgment due to extrinsic mistake. (Cruz v. Fagor America, Inc. (2007) 146 Cal. App. 4th 488, 503). Mr. Schanzer has shown that he has a meritorious case by the affirmative defense of self-defense. Mr. Schanzer has shown a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense prior to the entry of his default by his lack of knowledge of this case. Mr. Schanzer filed this motion very promptly after learning that his default and default judgment had been entered. Any money or property received by Mr. Saidin or his counsel or anyone on their behalf in collecting on the judgment must be returned to Mr. Schanzer through his counsel immediately. Mr. Schanzer must file his answer no later than January 29, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Schanzer is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Pour Guys Soma, Llc Dba Tempest Bar & Grill Resturant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT POUR GUYS SOMA LLC DBA TEMPEST BAR & GRILL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Defendant Pour Guys SOMA LLC's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication of six specified issues is denied in its entirety. The doctrine of primary assumption of the risk does not apply to the facts of this case as a matter of law. Nalwa v. Cedar Fair, L.P. (2012) 55 Cal.4th 1148 explained that the doctrine applies to sporting and "recreational activities." The purpose of the doctrine is "to avoid chilling vigorous participation in or sponsorship of recreational activities." (Nalwa, 55 Cal.4th at 1156.) Mr. Rousssos was not engaging in a sporting or recreational activity and there is no public policy to encourage zealous participation in street fights. Morris v. De La Torre (2005) 36 Cal.4th 260 held that there is a special relationship between a bar and its patrons and a bar owes a duty to take such appropriate action as reasonable to protect a patron from ongoing criminal conduct that occurs in the presence of bar employees. There is a triable dispute whether the stabbing occurred "off premises." There is evidence that Pour Guys had control over the area where the altercation occurred. Pour Guys runs the "Box Kitchen." The fight occurred on the sidewalk by the kitchen. Patrons go to the sidewalk to order their food from the kitchen's window. (Bright Depo., 33:1-3). Pour Guys' lease provides that Pour Guys agreed to clean the sidewalk. (Braun Dec., Ex. 8, par. 27). Part of Mr. Lopez's security duties was to shoo away persons that slept on the sidewalk and/or hassled kitchen patrons. (Lopez Depo., 48:21-49:2; 51:3-8; 53:11-21.) Pour Guys even had a bench on the sidewalk. (Lopez Depo., 190:16-191:5.) There is a triable dispute whether the bar was "closed" when the incident occurred. Mr. Roussos left the bar at 1:50 an. There is evidence that the fight occurred shortly thereafter when the bar was in the process of closing down. (Bright Depo., 33:16-34:13; Roussos Dec., pars. 8-11.) There is a triable dispute whether the harm to Mr. Roussos was foreseeable. There is evidence that patrons were milling about at closing time when the first fight ensued. Mr. Bright went outside to break up the first fight. It was foreseeable that more altercations such as the one that injured Mr. Roussos could arise. It was a fluid situation. However, Pour Guy's security guard remained in the bar (Braun Dec., Ex. 5, Form Interrogatory response 12.1.) There is also a triable dispute whether Pour Guys'/Mr. Lopez's inaction was a substantial factor in causing Mr. Roussos' injuries. Pour Guys fails to maintain its summary adjudication burden on issues 3-6. Mr. Roussos alleges that Pour Guys is liable for Mr. Lopez's nonfeasance or misfeasance. Pour Boys does not present evidence addressing those theories of liability. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Roussos is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC VS. BEN ESCOBAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Intervision Systems, Llc'S Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (Part 3 Of 4 For Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 2, Plaintiff Intervision Systems, LLC'S Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (Part 3 Of 4 For Tentative Ruling) RPD to Fouldegar Set One, Nos. 1-13: GRANTED, the requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is relevant, and not vague and ambiguous under the circumstances (no discussion is offered by responding party in his briefing why he supposedly cannot understand what "InterVision's business" is or who any of their clients were as of the time he left InterVision's employment). Responding party's asserted privacy rights are outweighed by plaintiff's interest in discovery of relevant evidence and those privacy rights should be protected by the provisions of the existing stipulated protective order. To the extent responding party appears to be claiming that all responsive documents have previously been produced and/or that he has conducted a diligent search and is affirming that he indeed has no such responsive documents, the supplemental statement that "investigation continues" equivocates and casts doubt upon the previous affirmation. If a thorough and complete - i.e., diligent - search has been performed as required, then the further response should plainly so state, without equivocation - if further investigation is required, then the responding party must now diligently complete that search and provide further substantive, definitive, verified responses without objections within 21 days of this Order. Interrogatories to Fouldegar Set Two, Nos. 8 and 13: DENIED as to Interrogatory No. 8. Responding Party's answer is responsive and unequivocal. A verification is ordered to be provided within 7 days of the date of this Order if one has not yet been provided. GRANTED as to Interrogatory No. 13. The requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is relevant, and on the record presented, plaintiff's interest outweighs the asserted burden. Further substantive verified responses without objections are to be provided within 21 days of this Order. Interrogatories to Lucero Set Two Nos. 10 and 11: GRANTED, the requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Interrogatory No. 11 is not vague or overbroad, and not unduly burdensome in relation to plaintiff's right to pursue evidence to prove its allegations. Further substantive verified responses without objections are to be provided within 21 days of this Order. Nothing in this Order is intended to preclude the parties from utilizing the provisions of their previously entered Stipulated Protective Order (a copy of which has not been provided by either party on this motion). (continue on part 4) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC VS. BEN ESCOBAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery #2
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC's Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (part 1 of 4) Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Requests for Production of Documents ("RPD") to Evotek Set Two Nos. 22-32: GRANTED, the requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is relevant, and privacy assertions for a corporation are more limited; defendants do not explain why the provisions of the already stipulated protective order including the "attorneys eyes only" provision does not sufficiently satisfy those concerns; this Court on balance concludes they do. Similarly, adequate time limitations are given in the Request, they are not overbroad. Responsive documents and a verified further substantive response without objections are to be produced within 21 days of the date of this Order. Interrogatories to Evotek, Set One, Nos. 3-12 GRANTED, the requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is relevant, and corporate privacy rights are more limited than individual's privacy rights; defendants do not explain why the provisions of the already stipulated protective order does not sufficiently satisfy those concerns and this Court on balance concludes they do. Similarly, adequate time limitations are given in the Request, they are not overbroad. No discussion of the asserted third-party privacy rights is provided (No. 12), nor is the assertion discussed in defense counsel's declaration (this issue was not well-briefed by either party: nevertheless, in the absence of an actual individual asserting a privacy right, the limited nature of the interrogatory to simply of an offer of employment was made without regard to the proposed terms, plaintiff's interest in proving their allegations outweighs the limited and unsubstantiated privacy interest asserted.) "Solicited employees" shall be deemed to mean solicited them to be employed by Evotek (No. 10). Further verified substantive responses without objections are to be provided within 21 days of the date of this Order. (continue on part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC VS. BEN ESCOBAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Intervision Systems, Llc'S Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (Part 4 Of 4 For Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 2, Plaintiff Intervision Systems, LLC'S Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (Part 4 Of 4 For Tentative Ruling) This Court finds that meet and confer efforts by the parties were meager, requiring this Court to decide some matters which were capable of resolution without Court intervention had the parties' counsel actually done the work required to focus on each discovery item instead of defaulting to motion practice. However, the moving party made clear its view of the discovery dispute in its meet and confer e-mails of November 12 and 16, whereas responding parties' counsel response was simply "Understood and thanks," without offering any meet and confer responding to the assertion that objections had been waived or that plaintiff believed responsive documents either had not been produced as required or may have been destroyed. Defense counsel, as the party asserting objections and responding in a very limited fashion, had an obligation to offer some explanation in a responding meet and confer; had they proffered what they eventually proffered in responding to this Motion, then perhaps some informal resolution as to some of the discovery items presented here might have been forthcoming. That did not occur, and plaintiff was within its rights to proceed with its motion. However, it is the policy of this Court to strenuously urge counsel to actually engage in genuine good faith meet and confer rather than cursory meet and confer. The Court finds that defendants' opposition is without substantial justification in most respects for the reasons discussed above and that defense counsel did not engage in good faith meet and confer. However, CCP 2023.040 requires that the Notice of Motion identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought. As the moving party failed to so identify any particular party or attorney in its Notice, sanctions are denied without prejudice as to any potential further, properly noticed, sanctions motion. As the prevailing party, Plaintiff shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this tentative ruling, [other details, if any], and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INTERVISION SYSTEMS, LLC VS. BEN ESCOBAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Intervision Systems, Llc'S Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (Part 2 Of 4 For Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 2, Plaintiff Intervision Systems, LLC'S Motion To Compel Discovery #2 (Part 2 Of 4 For Tentative Ruling) Form Interrogatories to Evotek Set One Nos. 12.1-12.3, 13.1, and 15.1: GRANTED, the requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is relevant, and while the term "INCIDENT" as defined here could be more precise, nevertheless, under the circumstances of this matter, it seems readily apparent that the term refers to any and all incidents of an Evotek employee or officer contacting an InterVision employee with the intent to discuss possible employment by Evotek. The asserted attorney-work product objection has been waived for failure to timely raise it; again, if defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay, or can file a belated motion to be excused from their waiver; however, under the record presented here by defendants' declarations insufficient grounds are presented to excuse defendants' failure to timely object. To the extent a panoply of additional objections are presented in a preface to defendants' written answers, the Court notes that Cal. Civ. Proc. Code § 2030.210(a) requires an "objection to the particular interrogatory." Further, verified, substantive and comprehensive responses, without objections, are required within 21 days of the date of this Order. RPD To Escobar Set Two Nos. 30-41: GRANTED, the requested material is relevant and objections were not timely, and no explanation of the asserted "excusable neglect" is given. If defendants could not satisfy the extended due date, then they could have sought a further extension, or filed a motion for a protective order showing good cause for the delay. Even if the responses were deemed to be timely, however, the requested material is relevant, and not vague and ambiguous under the circumstances, and responding party's asserted privacy rights are outweighed by plaintiff's interest in discovery of relevant evidence and those privacy rights should be protected by the provisions of the existing stipulated protective order. To the extent responding party appears to be claiming that all responsive documents have previously been produced and that no further responsive documents exist, then the required further response should plainly so state.. Further substantive verified responses without objections are to be provided within 21 days of this Order. Interrogatories to Escobar Set Two No. 8: GRANTED. A further substantive verified response without limitation as to subject matter of the communications with any InterVision employees shall be provided within 10 days of the date of this Order. (continue on part 3) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MIZRACHI ET AL VS. SEYMOUR HOLTZMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 15, DEFENDANTS SEYMOUR HOLTZMAN, HOLTZMAN FAMILY PARTNERSHIP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendants Seymour Holtzman and Holtzman Family Partnership's motion to quash based on lack of personal jurisdiction is denied and their motion to stay this action based on inconvenient forum is granted. This case is stayed pending the completion of proceedings in Florida on the claims alleged by plaintiffs Joseph Mizrachi and JAL Group, L.P. Plaintiffs have demonstrated that defendants have sufficient minimum contacts with California to warrant specific jurisdiction over defendants in this case. Defendants purposefully availed themselves of the benefits of California through their extensive dealings with plaintiffs in acquiring interests in the Brentwood entities which involved retaining California attorney James Smith and Kilpatrick Townsend & Stockton LLP. Notwithstanding that this court may exercise personal jurisdiction over defendants, plaintiffs' claims should be litigated in Florida, not in California. Florida is a suitable alternative forum and the parties are currently engaged in litigation in that state. The public and private factors strongly favor litigation of plaintiffs' claims in Florida where the individual parties reside. California has minimal interest in this case which does not involve any California residents and is based on an alleged oral contract that, although it concerns payment of fees to California residents, was allegedly entered into and allegedly breached in Florida. There is no reason to believe that any party would be disadvantaged by litigating plaintiffs' claims in Florida and plaintiffs do not so argue. Apparently the parties are not aware that the default of defendants was entered on December 12, 2018, prior to the defendants' filing of this motion. Based on the papers filed on this motion, the court on its own motion and in the interests of justice and consistent with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits, vacates the defaults entered against defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order entitled "Order Vacating Entry of Defaults and Staying this Case Based on Inconvenient Forum" which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAULINE ROBINSON VS. HANSON BRIDGETT LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hanson Bridgetts Llps Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Production Of Documenmts
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT HANSON BRIDGETT LLP's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Production Of Documenmts The pro tem judge recuses himself from hearing this matter based upon his familial relationship with a partner for one of the parties' counsel of record. The motion is continued to Jan. 25, 2019. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL REITERMAN VS. DOES 1 TO 10, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Michael Reiterman For Expedited Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 17, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL REITERMAN's Motion For Expedited Discovery Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Good cause appearing, the motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff has established the prima facie elements of his claims for defamation and related torts and has sufficiently established efforts to obtain the required identifying information through less intrusive means, and to serve notice of this motion on the involved parties. Under the circumstances presented, the motion is granted and plaintiff may have leave pursuant to CCP Section 2025.210 to proceed with deposition subpoenas to the following parties: Reddit, and Automattic Inc. As the prevailing party, Plaintiff shall prepare a form of the order recapitulating this tentative ruling, [other details, if any], and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, the prevailing party shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
